summary: The implementation covers most requirements including product catalog, shopping cart, checkout flow, and Internet Identity authentication. However, there is a critical gap: the backend does not implement an getOrdersByUser() or equivalent method for authenticated users to retrieve their order history (REQ-4 AC), and there is no actual order history display implementation despite a placeholder page existing. Additionally, the generated image assets referenced in REQ-7 are not present in the diff, and the backend's order storage does not properly capture timestamps (hardcoded to 0).

missing_requirements.requirement: Authenticated users can view their order history
missing_requirements.reason: Backend (backend/main.mo) only has getOrder(orderId) which retrieves a single order by ID, but provides no method to retrieve all orders for an authenticated user. The frontend OrderHistory.tsx explicitly notes 'Backend doesn't have a method to get user's order history' and shows only a placeholder UI.

missing_requirements.requirement: Generated images are placed in frontend/public/assets/generated directory
missing_requirements.reason: The diff shows no added files in frontend/public/assets/generated/. While the frontend code references these images (hero-banner.dim_1920x600.png and product placeholders), the actual image files are not present in the file changes.

missing_requirements.requirement: Orders are stored with user identity, items, and timestamp
missing_requirements.reason: While the Order type in backend/main.mo includes a timestamp field, the checkout() function hardcodes it to 0 instead of capturing the actual timestamp at order creation time.
The products Map in backend/main.mo is not declared as stable storage. Without stable or persistent storage declarations, the product data will be lost on canister upgrades.
